questions.q: What is the purpose of problem-solving in computer science? Explain the importance of problem-solving with a suitable example.
questions.answer: Problem-solving is a crucial aspect of computer science as it enables developers to design, implement, and optimize solutions to real-world problems. The purpose of problem-solving is to identify, analyze, and develop a solution to a problem using algorithms and data structures. For instance, consider a scenario where a hospital wants to develop a system to manage patient records. A developer would need to identify the problem (in this case, managing patient records), analyze the requirements, and design an algorithm to solve the problem. The importance of problem-solving can be seen in its ability to improve efficiency, reduce errors, and enhance the overall quality of a system. By applying problem-solving skills, developers can create innovative solutions that meet the needs of users.
marking_scheme: Explain the purpose of problem-solving in computer science (1 mark)
Provide an example to demonstrate the importance of problem-solving (2 marks)
Describe the benefits of problem-solving (2 marks)

questions.q: What are the steps involved in the problem-solving process? Explain each step with a suitable example.
questions.answer: The problem-solving process involves the following steps: (1) Identify the problem, (2) Analyze the problem, (3) Develop a solution, (4) Implement the solution, and (5) Test and evaluate the solution. For instance, consider a scenario where a student wants to develop a system to track their assignments. The problem-solving process would involve: (1) Identifying the problem (tracking assignments), (2) Analyzing the requirements (what information needs to be tracked), (3) Developing a solution (designing an algorithm to track assignments), (4) Implementing the solution (writing the code), and (5) Testing and evaluating the solution (verifying that the system works as expected). Each step is crucial in ensuring that the problem is solved efficiently and effectively.
marking_scheme: List the steps involved in the problem-solving process (1 mark)
Explain each step with a suitable example (3 marks)
Describe the importance of each step (1 mark)

questions.q: What is the difference between a problem and a solution? Provide an example to illustrate the distinction.
questions.answer: A problem is an issue or situation that needs to be addressed, whereas a solution is a plan or set of actions that resolves the problem. To illustrate the distinction, consider a scenario where a company wants to reduce its energy consumption. The problem is the high energy bills, and the solution is to install energy-efficient lighting and adjust the working hours. The problem is the issue that needs to be addressed, whereas the solution is the plan to resolve the issue. A problem is often characterized by uncertainty, complexity, and ambiguity, whereas a solution is a clear and well-defined plan.
marking_scheme: Provide an example to illustrate the distinction (2 marks)
Describe the characteristics of a problem (1 mark)

questions.q: What is the importance of analysis in the problem-solving process? Explain the role of analysis in identifying and defining the problem.
questions.answer: Analysis is a critical step in the problem-solving process as it enables developers to understand the problem, identify the key issues, and define the requirements. The importance of analysis can be seen in its ability to ensure that the problem is properly understood, the requirements are accurately defined, and the solution is effective and efficient. Analysis involves breaking down complex problems into smaller, manageable parts, identifying the root causes of the problem, and gathering relevant data and information. By applying analysis skills, developers can create a detailed understanding of the problem, which is essential for developing a effective solution.
marking_scheme: Explain the importance of analysis in the problem-solving process (2 marks)
Describe the role of analysis in identifying and defining the problem (2 marks)
Provide an example to illustrate the importance of analysis (1 mark)

questions.q: What is the role of creativity in the problem-solving process? Explain how creativity can be applied to develop innovative solutions.
questions.answer: Creativity plays a vital role in the problem-solving process as it enables developers to think outside the box, come up with novel ideas, and develop innovative solutions. Creativity involves applying imagination, intuition, and expertise to identify new opportunities and develop unique solutions. By applying creativity skills, developers can create solutions that are not only effective but also innovative and sustainable. For instance, consider a scenario where a company wants to develop a system to improve customer engagement. A creative approach would involve brainstorming new ideas, such as using artificial intelligence to personalize customer interactions or developing a mobile app to enhance customer experience. By applying creativity, developers can develop solutions that meet the needs of users and stay ahead of the competition.
marking_scheme: Explain the role of creativity in the problem-solving process (2 marks)
Describe how creativity can be applied to develop innovative solutions (2 marks)
Provide an example to illustrate the importance of creativity (1 mark)
